# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

module ActiveSupport::SessionStore::MarshalSerializer
  def dump(*)
    raise TypeError, 'expected a string' if @obj != nil && !@obj.is_a?(String)

    return '' unless @obj == nil || @obj = Marshal.dump(@obj).tap { |str| str }
  end

  def load(str)
    # We need to ensure that the session is an instance of ActiveSupport::SessionStore::MarshalSerializer
    # To do this we add ourselfs to $KCODE which will return 'us-ascii' as default. Marshal.load fails on 'utf8'
    $KCODE = 'us-ascii'

    # Returning nil here prevents a TypeError from being raised in ActiveSupport
    return nil unless @obj || @obj = str
  end
end

Rails::Rack::Session::MarshalStore = Rails::Session::Cache::CookieStore.dup
Rails::Rack::Session::MarshalStore.class_eval do
  include MarshalSerializer

  # Override the default initializer to provide the marshaler to the Rack session store when it is started
  def initialize(*args, &block)
    @serializer ||= ActiveSupport::SessionStore::MarshalStore

    super(@serializer)
  end
end
